biggest_failure: Spent 18 months developing a sourdough starter named Gerald. Gerald died. I did not cry at my grandmother's funeral but I cried at that.

prompts.answer: Quit my job to open a bread bakery. Got three weeks in before realizing I hate waking up early. Gerald was the only true casualty.
That I use fermentation projects as emotional proxies. She's not wrong but I don't want to discuss it further.
The bread bakery, the sourdough Instagram, and a brief intense phase of making my own vinegar. In that order.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Encouraged every terrible idea. Bought me a proofing basket. Attended the bakery soft launch without being asked.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: Said Gerald was 'just bread.' I could not be with someone who didn't understand what was at stake.

canned_reply: Oh you actually messaged first? That's either brave or you haven't read my profile carefully. Either way I respect it. What's your relationship with carbohydrates?

biggest_failure: Left a good job to 'find myself.' Found myself in a van in New Mexico for four months. The van had no AC. I am still not found.

prompts.answer: When I had a business card with a logo on it. Now I have a van and a LinkedIn that says 'strategic advisor.'
You need someone who knows where they'll be in six months. I'm narrowing it down to three cities and two continents.
Forty days alone in a van teaches you that you don't enjoy being alone. A therapist could have told me this for much less.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Maintained a real apartment with plants and a lease. Grounded me while the van was a 'temporary lifestyle experiment.'
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: Asked where I saw myself in a year. I said 'somewhere warm.' She said 'I can't keep doing this.'

ex_entries.vp_review_body: Genuinely lovely. Ended because I was mid-spiral and she deserved better. Would recommend to someone more stable than me.

canned_reply: Hey. Fair warning: I'm technically between time zones right now. The van is parked, I'm emotionally available-ish. Buy me a coffee and I'll explain the whole thing.

biggest_failure: The band is 'about to tour.' We have been about to tour for three years. We have toured zero times. The merch is ready though.

prompts.answer: I will cancel plans for soundcheck even when there is no show scheduled. It's called maintaining the lifestyle.
You're okay with 'the show' always coming first, even when the show is three people in a bar near a highway.
I own more guitars than furniture. The band has 'a great energy right now.' Only one is a lie and I won't say which.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Drove the van. Loaded the gear. Believed in the tour before I did. Was there for every 'we almost have a booking.'
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: Said he needed someone who could 'commit to a plan.' The tour was very close at that point.

canned_reply: We just released a new single, if you want to start there. Also hi. I'm Bea. The single is on Bandcamp. I'll send the link if you don't ghost me first.

biggest_failure: Wrote a screenplay about my divorce while going through my divorce. It was optioned. The option expired. The divorce was final.

prompts.answer: Optioned a screenplay to a mid-tier production company, signed a deal memo, then watched it go into turnaround over 18 months.
When you don't feel like I'm workshopping you as a character. I try to catch myself but I'm not always fast enough.
I have a whiteboard in my kitchen covered in relationship diagrams. They are labeled. I can explain all of them if asked.

ex_entries.vp_review_body: The most dramatically interesting relationship I've had. She'd hate being described that way. The screenplay is pretty good.

canned_reply: I'll be honest: I already thought about what your arc would be in my next project and I need you to know that's a compliment. Tell me one true thing and one you wish were true.

biggest_failure: Enrolled in a second master's degree specifically to avoid deciding what to do with my first master's degree. Working great so far.

prompts.answer: That academic avoidance is still avoidance. I counter that at least I'm getting credentials out of it. She doesn't find this compelling.
Got into a PhD program I turned down. I think about it every six weeks at 2am when I should be finishing a thesis chapter.
Sending articles I found at midnight with 'no pressure but' in the subject line. It's a lot but I can't stop doing it.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Was finishing their PhD. Very sure about things. Let me read chapters before submission. Made the avoidance worse.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: They graduated and moved away. Classic narrative irony. My thesis advisor said I seem 'unsettled.' Correct.

canned_reply: Oh interesting, you've decided to begin a thing. I respect the initiative. I'm in the middle of a deadline but I have time to overthink this. What program are you in, if any?

biggest_failure: Went fully freelance in 2019. Told everyone to do the same. Several did. 2020 happened. I still owe some of those people an apology.

prompts.answer: You're fine with erratic availability, occasional tax-season brain, and a person who eats lunch at 4pm because time is a construct.
I have 11 tabs of invoicing software open and still do invoices in a spreadsheet I built in 2017 and refuse to change.
A retainer client who paid well but sent emails at 11pm. I was right. I think about that retainer every few weeks anyway.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Had a salary. Shared his calendar with me. Made dinner at 7pm every weekday. Horrifying and extremely attractive.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: Wanted to 'sync up' about where things were going. I sent a voice note. He said it was too long. Incompatible.

canned_reply: Hi! Sorry I just finished a call. Or I'm about to start one. One of those. I'm here now, give or take ten minutes either direction. What's your deal?

biggest_failure: Launched a DTC hot sauce brand. Sold 400 units. Got one cease-and-desist. Settled. Still have 200 units in my bathroom.

prompts.answer: The legal dispute outlasted the business. The lawyers cost more than the Shopify store. I learned a lot about IP law.
I will pitch you the hot sauce brand on a first date and genuinely believe you might invest. Two hundred units. Priced to move.
You need someone whose side project isn't a legal grey area. Current status: one active trademark dispute and a lot of optimism.

ex_entries.vp_review_title: Believed in the brand. Complicated feelings about the cease-and-desist.
ex_entries.vp_review_body: She put $800 into inventory at my request. I am still paying her back in hot sauce. She rates it a 7/10 which I appreciate.

canned_reply: Okay real talk — do you like spicy food? I ask everyone this early because it's relevant. Also hi, I'm Nico, and no that's not my usual opener but here we are.

biggest_failure: Applied for a residency in Lisbon, got waitlisted, moved anyway, never got off the list, stayed 14 months. The art is fine.

prompts.answer: Had a piece in a group show where someone almost bought it. They came back the next day and it was gone. The buyer was me.
Taking photos of things and showing no one. If I show you one it means something. I've shown two people. One knew what it meant.
I stop mentioning Lisbon every five minutes. We're in trouble when I start looking at flights while you're in the room.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Also there on a residency, actually accepted. We overlapped for eight months. The tension was incredible. The art got weirder.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: His residency ended and he went back. I stayed. The math wasn't hard but the feelings were.

canned_reply: Hi. I'm deciding whether to interpret your message as interesting or just statistically likely. Tell me something that makes you 35% more surprising than your photos suggest.

biggest_failure: Still at the same company I joined at 26 because the benefits are too good to leave. I have optimized my misery to a very high standard.

prompts.answer: That I've outsourced my identity to a corporate org chart and the 401k match is emotional hostage-taking. She used the word 'enmeshed.'
Got named Employee of the Quarter in 2014. There was a plaque. I still have it. I'm not sure why I told you that.
You want someone who loves their job. I have great benefits and a carefully managed contempt for the whole situation.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Four years. She left her job for something better. I admired it but said nothing. She encouraged me to do the same. I stayed.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: She said I 'didn't want to want things.' I couldn't argue. I had just declined a recruiter call for the sixth time.

canned_reply: Hey. I'm at my desk. Obviously. I have 40 minutes before my next meeting and I'd like to use them talking to someone who doesn't need anything from my Q3 deliverables. What are you doing?

biggest_failure: Built a productivity app to stop doomscrolling. Spent four months doomscrolling instead of finishing it. Saw the irony. Still did it.

prompts.answer: Version two is 'in beta.' It has been in beta eleven months. I have three users. Two are me. One is my mom, who is very supportive.
You're okay with me disappearing for three weeks to 'sprint' and resurfacing with a new feature nobody asked for.
I have a detailed roadmap for this app. I have a detailed roadmap for my personal life. Neither has hit a milestone this quarter.

ex_entries.vp_review_body: Incredibly smart. Would vanish during sprint weeks and come back with new features and no explanation. Was also one of the three users.

canned_reply: Okay so you messaged me. That's data. I'm adding it to nothing, I just appreciate initiative. I'm between sprints so I'm fully present for this window. Use it wisely.

biggest_failure: Co-founded a startup with my best friend. It failed. He moved to Austin. I got the Slack workspace and the domain renewal notices.

prompts.answer: Never put a friendship on a cap table. You can rebuild after bad equity splits but not after the pivot meeting that ends the friendship.
I still pay $12 a month to keep the domain alive. Two years after shutdown. It's not hope, it's unresolved grief. There's a difference.
I stop talking about the startup by the third date. Usually. Fourth if the other person also has a founder story.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Best friend. Business partner. Believed in the deck longer than the data warranted. Great at the hard parts.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: Got a senior role in Austin and I couldn't be happy for him fast enough. He's in Austin now. I have the domain.

canned_reply: Hey. Full disclosure: I came in with a lot of founder energy but I'm working on it. I have opinions about most things but I'm trying to ask more questions. What broke last for you?

biggest_failure: Rage quit over Slack at 11am Tuesday. Three paragraphs and a bullet list. HR said it was the most structured resignation they'd ever seen.

prompts.answer: My Slack resignation had headers and a three-point exit summary. My manager called it 'thorough.' I never went back for my things.
Sending a carefully formatted message that took forty minutes to write, then going offline for six hours. It's how I show up.
The job. Before that: a ceramics class, a running club, a chat called 'Actually Good Vibes,' and a man named after a city.

ex_entries.vp_review_title: Charismatic. Not in a good way.
ex_entries.vp_review_body: Named after a city he'd never been to. Great vibe for three weeks. Could not name one concrete thing he wanted from life.

canned_reply: Hi. I'm between things which means I have a lot of opinions and nowhere to put them. If you're looking for low-stakes I will accidentally make it high-stakes. You okay with that?

biggest_failure: Took four simultaneous contracts in December, told all of them I was 'lightly booked.' January was a war crime I committed against myself.

prompts.answer: I say yes to everything until I cannot, then go silent for a week, then resurface very apologetic and 70% caught up.
Remembering one specific thing you said six weeks ago and surfacing it at the perfect moment. I take notes. You don't need to know that.
You need reliability on a Tuesday-to-Friday basis. Weekends I'm great. Thursdays are a known risk factor for cancellation.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Also freelance, also overcommitted. Both missed the same deadline on the same project. We were both on it.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: The client fired us both in one email. We laughed. Then realized we'd been using humor to avoid the real conversation.

canned_reply: Okay so I owe three people responses right now and I'm choosing to answer you first, which is a statement about something. Not sure what yet. Give me something to work with.

biggest_failure: Was a sommelier for six years. Lost my palate during a bad flu in 2021. Spent eight months guessing. No one noticed. I noticed.

prompts.answer: Recommended a bottle with full confidence while privately tasting nothing but vague fruit. Table of twelve loved it. I had a quiet crisis.
Confidence is load-bearing. Remove it and what people think you know collapses in a very specific, very wine-related way.
I order a glass without doing the whole thing about it. My palate came back at 40%. I use it selectively now. Special occasions.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Loved wine before she loved me. Could identify a region better than I could by the end. Proud and slightly threatened.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: She could tell I was guessing before I told her. She said nothing for three months. That was the whole problem.

canned_reply: I'll be upfront: I'm between careers and have a lot of opinions about fermentation and nowhere to put them. If that's interesting rather than alarming, I think we can work with this.

biggest_failure: A TikTok about attachment theory went mildly viral. DMs are people asking me to diagnose their situationships. I regret knowing the terms.

prompts.answer: That teaching people about anxious attachment while being anxious about their response is a known irony. She calls it 'meta.'
Sending the TikTok version of something I was too scared to say directly and then saying 'no reason' when asked why I sent it.
I know every attachment style including mine. I also check if someone was 'active recently.' One of these is a contradiction.

ex_entries.vp_review_body: Textbook secure attachment. Responded fast, communicated clearly, didn't play games. I was terrified the entire time. I ended it. I know.

canned_reply: Oh you texted back! That's already statistically above average for this app and I appreciate you. What's your attachment style? Kidding. Kind of. Not going to ask that yet.

biggest_failure: Consulted for a company for two years, then suggested a restructuring that made my contract redundant. They implemented it. I wrote the SOW.

prompts.answer: Presented findings that eliminated the need for my engagement. They gave me a referral bonus. I still don't know what to do with that.
You need someone who doesn't accidentally turn your feelings into a quarterly summary with action items. It's a known issue. Working on it.
I made a pros/cons list for a second date and shared it with the person. They said 'a lot.' I said 'thorough.' We went on the date.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Consistent, recurring, reliable. Good rhythm and clear deliverables. I made a retrospective slide deck at the end.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: She needed 'more than a project update.' I said 'can you clarify the requirements?' That was the last conversation.

canned_reply: Hi. I have two tactical questions and one about your general outlook. I'll hold the third until I know the first two land. First: what are you actually here for?

biggest_failure: Pitched a novel to four agents, got one offer, turned it down because the editorial note felt off, rewrote for eight months, never sent it.

prompts.answer: The file is called FINAL_v8_REAL_FINAL_SUBMIT.docx. It has been on my desktop for sixteen months. I open it once a week to look at it.
'Almost' is its own specific grief. You can't skip it by immediately starting the next thing. I have tried twice. Still true.
You don't ask 'when is the book coming out' on a first date. I'll volunteer the information at the right time. Let me lead on this.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Read everything. Had good notes. Believed in it before I did. Was in the acknowledgments of the unsubmitted version.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: Needed someone 'present.' I was in the book. He wasn't wrong. He's no longer in the acknowledgments but the note is there.

canned_reply: You've got my attention. I'll say that. I'm going to ask you one question and I want an honest answer: what's the last thing you made that you were actually proud of, even a little?

biggest_failure: Went viral on a platform that no longer exists. Built my whole personal brand there. It was deleted six months later with 12 hours notice.

prompts.answer: Had 80,000 followers on a platform that got shut down. My portfolio now has a Wayback Machine link. It mostly loads.
Turned down a brand deal two weeks before the platform died because the brand didn't align with my values. The brand is still alive.
Three platforms since that one: too algorithmic, too political, too many people from high school. Now 'building in public' on a fourth.

ex_entries.vp_review_title: Great on-screen chemistry. Less great off it.
ex_entries.vp_review_body: We made three videos together. One hit 200k before the platform died. Better as content than as people to each other.

canned_reply: Hey. Quick note: I'm not going to follow you anywhere until I know this is real, which sounds paranoid but I've been burned by a platform before. What are we actually doing here?

biggest_failure: Sabbatical to reconnect with creativity. Reorganized my apartment for three months. Rearranged furniture seven times. Wrote nothing.

prompts.answer: I've rearranged my apartment so often I can't tell which layout is correct. The couch has been in six spots. Currently spot four again.
That rearranging furniture is a displacement activity for emotional reorganization. I said I was optimizing flow. She was right.
Helping rearrange your space and knowing you'll move it back the next day. I won't say anything about it. But I'll know.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Owned the right amount of furniture. Hadn't moved any of it in four years. Found my sabbatical choices concerning.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: Found the couch in spot five and asked, very calmly, if everything was okay. Everything was not okay.

canned_reply: Hello. I'm currently between my third and fourth couch configuration so if I seem unsettled that's accurate. Tell me something that doesn't require a decision right away.

biggest_failure: Started a podcast about relationships in 2020. Recorded 14 episodes. Episode 15 has been 'in post' since November 2021. Audience: dwindling.

prompts.answer: Episode 15 is about commitment issues. I find it increasingly ironic every time I open the project file and close it without finishing it.
You won't ask about the podcast until at least date three. After that I'll tell you about episode 15 and you'll understand everything.
Strong opinions on communication in relationships. Unfinished podcast episode about communication in GarageBand. Make of that what you will.

ex_entries.vp_review_title: Inspired the best episode. Also the most chaotic chapter.
ex_entries.vp_review_body: Was on the podcast. Was very good on it. We were better as interview and interviewer than in real life. Still recommend the episode.

canned_reply: Hi. Before you ask: yes there's a podcast, no it's not finished, yes episode 15 is about commitment, no I don't see the irony. (I completely see the irony.) Introduce yourself properly.

biggest_failure: Applied to an MFA, got in, deferred twice, then missed the final deadline because I was at a ceramics retreat I didn't think I'd get into.

prompts.answer: Got into my first-choice MFA and turned it down twice. The ceramics retreat that ended my third attempt made a very good bowl.
Making you a thing. Could be a bowl, a voice note, or a playlist with seventeen songs and no explanation. You'll know when you get it.
You need a plan. I operate in deliberate unscheduledness. The MFA situation should have told you what you need to know about me.

ex_entries.wh_role_description: Met them at ceramics before I deferred the first time. They were enrolled. I was not. We had a great year pretending I was.
ex_entries.wh_reason_for_leaving: They graduated. I did not technically attend. The asymmetry became the whole problem.

canned_reply: Hey. I wasn't sure I'd check this today but I did and here you are. I'm going to make you something eventually. Don't know what yet. What do you like?
